data.text: I’ll use the HELIX build workflow because this is an execute-bead task with explicit DDx acceptance criteria. I’m going to inspect the named docgraph and frontend test areas first, then make the narrow implementation and tests needed for the bead.

input.command: /bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' /home/erik/.ddx/plugins/helix/.agents/skills/helix-build/SKILL.md && pwd && git status --short && rg -n \"func .*Graph|findMarkdownFiles|doc\\.Path|documents|document\\(\" cli/internal/docgraph cli/internal/server -S"

data.text: The initial scan shows there are already dirty skill-directory changes unrelated to this bead. I’ll leave those untouched and focus only on the docgraph, GraphQL, and frontend test files named by the acceptance criteria.

The relevant implementation is present in this checkout, but one acceptance point is weaker than the contract: `documentByPath` currently rejects legacy absolute paths instead of tolerating safe ones. I’m going to tighten that and add a focused GraphQL test so AC #4 is provable.

data.text: I’m going to update the resolver normalization in one place: absolute paths that point inside the server working directory will be converted to the same relative path used by the graph, while traversal and outside-root absolute paths stay invalid. Then I’ll cover that with a GraphQL test.

The resolver now converts legacy absolute inputs under `WorkingDir` back to graph-relative paths and the new test asserts the returned GraphQL path is still relative. Next I’m running the Go packages that cover docgraph and GraphQL.

data.text: The Go module lives under `cli/`, so the first test command was pointed at the repo root by mistake and didn’t run. I’m rerunning from the module directory now, then I’ll run the frontend e2e spec for the documents page.

data.text: The commit hook failed before writing the commit because its `go-test` step invokes `-race` while this shell has cgo disabled. The frontend install also put `node_modules` under the Go module, which makes Go tooling scan extra packages, so I’m removing that generated dependency directory and retrying the commit with `CGO_ENABLED=1`.

input.command: /bin/zsh -lc 'rm -rf cli/internal/server/frontend/node_modules && CGO_ENABLED=1 git commit -m "fix(graphql): tolerate legacy absolute document paths [ddx-12cae4dd]"'

data.text: The pre-commit hook is now past build and lint; it’s still running the Go test suite under the hook. I’m waiting for that to finish before reporting the commit as complete.

data.text:
Implemented and committed:

`4f237bf1 fix(graphql): tolerate legacy absolute document paths [ddx-12cae4dd]`

What changed:
- `documentByPath` now normalizes legacy absolute paths under `WorkingDir` to graph-relative paths.
- Added GraphQL coverage for legacy absolute path resolution.
- Expanded docgraph fixture coverage to prove generic `worktrees/` subtrees are skipped.

Verified:
- `go test ./internal/docgraph ./internal/server`
- `bun run test:e2e -- e2e/documents.spec.ts --grep ddx-12cae4dd`
- Pre-commit hook passed with `CGO_ENABLED=1`.

Unrelated pre-existing skill-directory changes remain unstaged.
